events.title: Melody Artisans of the Crooked Thumb
Funk N Ting: DJ Mike Blenda & DJ Shafiq
Tori Amos w/Bartees Strange
events.description: Joining her on the road this year will be her longtime collaborators and “brothers” Jon Evans on bass and Ash Soan on drums. And for the first time, a trio of female indie singer-songwriters—unofficially called “Tori’s Angels”—Liv Gibson, Deni Hlavinka, and Hadley Kennary will be on hand to provide backing vocals for the new songs. 727-791-7400

events.title: Parrotfish
events.description: Ask four different Parrotfish fans to describe the band’s sound, and chances are you'll hear four different genres. The Tampa-born, Nashville-based quartet has built a catalog that comfortably slips between alternative rock, pop-punk, reggae, and funk, often within the span of a song or two. Despite the band's stylistic range, the music never feels like it's trying on different identities.—J.C. Roddy/Cigar City Sounds

events.title: Second Saturday Art Walk
events.description: From 5 p.m.-9 p.m. on Saturday, Jan. 14, the St. Pete Arts Alliance has coordinated a trolley that will connect riders to 40 different venues in a handful of districts as part of its monthly Second Saturday Artwalk. Service starts at stop No. 1 located at 2955 Central Ave. in St. Petersburg.

events.title: Summer at The Dalí
events.description: This summer, The Dalí is offering kids and students a reduced Gallery Admission rate of $12—an invitation to spend a day surrounded by art that sparks curiosity, changes perspectives and and rewards a closer look. Whether you’re local, visiting with friends or planning a family day out, The Dalí is a cultural must-see. 7278233767

events.title: Tampa Tarpons vs Clearwater Threshers
events.address: 3802 Dr Martin Luther King Jr Blvd, Tampa

events.title: GCP Summer One Acts 2026
events.description: Original short 10 minute plays chosen from hundreds of local & national submissions. Comedies, light dramas. Audience gets to vote for their favorites. 727- 322-0316

events.title: GÜD Essence: GÜD Yoga w/ Neidara
events.description: GŪD Yoga offers a deeply grounding yoga session. Sessions are led by Neidara Mathies (SmokenYoga), who has been teaching for over 14 years. Mathies is known for cultivating a serene and inviting atmosphere. Her classes blend mindful movement with intentional breathwork. Yogis will flow through a specially curated playlist and leave feeling balanced, centered, and completely renewed. This event is BYOW (Bring Your Own Wellness) format, and yoga mats and water are provided. All class attendees will receive exclusive in-store discounts. 954.723.9350

events.title: Willy Wonka Jr.
events.description: EVENT DETAILS Willy Wonka Jr. Presented by Progressive Arts Theater Friday, July 10, 2026 7:00 PM MainStage Theater Dunedin Community Center 1920 Pinehurst Road Dunedin, FL 34698 Tickets Children Under 12: $15 General Admission: $18 VIP Seating: $20 Children 3 and Under: Free About Progressive Arts Theater Progressive Arts Theater is a nonprofit performing arts organization based in Dunedin, Florida, dedicated to providing inclusive theater experiences for performers of all abilities. Through productions, classes, camps, scholarships, and community outreach programs, Progressive Arts Theater empowers children and adults to discover confidence, creativity, and connection through the arts. 828-226-4290

events.title: Kym Whitley
events.description: About the Artist: Kym Whitley is a multi-talented entertainer, rich with humor and heart. Kym is a Daytime Emmy-Nominated actress for her menacing role as Big Candi on “The Bay.” In addition, Kym is a two-time NAACP Image Award-winner for her “Two Funny Mamas” podcast. As an actress, author, comedian, and multi-award-winning podcaster, namely the 2023 Signal Award winner for her Audible podcast “Kym,” Whitley has proven her versatility and ability to captivate audiences across multiple mediums. Whitley’s latest roles in Bounce TV’s (recently acquired by Netflix), most-watched half-hour series “Act Your Age,” and the palpable magic created 813-864-4000

events.title: Lisa & The All Terrain Band
events.description: Fantastic cover band playing a wide range of tunes to get you on the dance floor! 941-251-3930

events.title: Crash Mitchell | Rancid Polecats | Boney Fiend
All Your Friends
events.description: An indie dance party for the ones who still romanticize a scratched iPod, American Spirits, and a blurry night soundtracked by bloghouse and post-punk revival. All Your Friends is at The Orpheum on 10 July 2026, bringing the sweat, synths, and nostalgia of the early 2010’s house party. This is your basement-floor flashback to tight jeans, smudged eyeliner, and nights that felt like a house show turned afterparty. 813-248-9500

events.title: All Your Friends
events.description: All Your Friends is the indie sleaze DJ dance party remixing all your favorite 2010s indie hits from Passion Pit, Grouplove, and LCD Soundsystem and more! Join the modern underground where bloghouse, post-punk revival, and internet-era club music collide. Grab your digital camera and relive the Tumblr and MySpace era where every night felt like a house show turned afterparty, soundtracked by The 1975, Phoenix, The Strokes, Tame Impala and more. 813-248-9500

events.title: Broadway Miscast - The Sequel
events.description: Join us for the revival of last year's smash hit comedy musical revue as we present all-new Broadway hits, performed by great singers who absolutely do NOT “fit” the role as originally written. From start to finish, this high energy musical revue will surprise and delight you at every turn. 8132654000

events.title: Back 2 School Health Clinic - North
events.description: The Back-2-School Health Clinics are returning this summer to help families across Hillsborough County prepare for the new school year. The clinics provide no-cost school physicals, routine immunizations, and Florida Form DH 680 immunization record transfers—services that are essential for school enrollment. A non-refundable $10 registration fee is required per family (not per child). Medical services are available by appointment only for children who are uninsured, underinsured, or enrolled in Medicaid. The clinics are designed to serve students entering Kindergarten, entering 7th grade, or those who are NEW to Hillsborough County Public or Charter Schools in any grade level. Clinics (813) 276-5671

events.title: Studio Saturdays
events.description: Put on your favorite pink attire! We’re taking inspiration from Yvette Mayorga’s The Magic Grasshopper and creating monochromatic pink artwork at our FREE pop-up studio in the Mary Alice McClendon Conservatory. We’ll bring the materials. You bring the fun! Designed for visitors of all ages, Studio Saturdays happen every second Saturday and include a free art-making activity. Enjoy Café Clementine, where members enjoy a 15% discount, and browse the MFA Shop for books, gifts and art supplies to take home. 7278962667

events.title: Cybertrucks for Kids Fundraiser - Benefiting Johns Hopkins Hospital & Presented by Personal Pitstop Express
events.description: Join Personal Pitstop Express for a one-of-a-kind community event as we bring Cybertruck owners together for an unforgettable cause to help the kids with Johns Hopkins. On Saturday, July 11 from 9:00 AM to noon, we will attempt to break the world record for the "Most Cybertrucks in a Row", with a goal of lining up 50+ Cybertrucks to cruise together through Tampa for an hour, attempting to raise $10,000. (813) 954-2814

events.title: Kidpreneur Summer Enrichment Day
events.description: The Kidpreneur Conference is a dynamic youth entrepreneurship experience where students ages 8–18 (3rd Grade-12th Grade) step into the role of real‑world creators and problem‑solvers. Participants explore a business scenario, develop a clear and confident elevator pitch, and showcase their innovative thinking in a supportive, high‑energy environment. The conference builds creativity, communication skills, and leadership, giving young entrepreneurs the space to express ideas, collaborate, and discover their potential. It’s hands‑on, inspiring, and designed to spark the next generation of CEOs, creators, and community changemakers. 727-300-9688

events.title: Color Collage Journal
events.description: We will spend 2.5 hours moving through the basics of each layout with detailed instructions, leaving extra time to add embellishments. Each kit will be individually curated making each one unique. All supplies are included; kits as well as shared materials (adhesive, scissors, washi, etc.) (813) 251-8096

events.title: Studio55: Ceramics
events.description: Use clay to build, mold, and sculpt three-dimensional works using coil building, slab building, and other hand-building techniques. All experience levels are welcome; beginners and up. Saturdays, Jul 11 – Aug 15 | 10:00 AM – 1:00 PM (6 classes) Ages 55+ | Beginner-Intermediate Level *Firing costs, essential tools, and glazes included. Clay available for purchase separately in studio. 813-274-8130

events.title: Back to School Summer Bash
events.description: ​This is a free family event hosted by Gulf Coast Community Church on Saturday, July 11, from 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM at 555 76th Avenue North, St. Petersburg, FL. Families can enjoy water slides, a splash zone, free lunch, games, and a free school supply giveaway while supplies last. This event is open to the entire community and is designed to help local families prepare for the upcoming school year. No registration is required. (727)522-7734

events.title: Neverne Covington: Into the Mystic Exhibition
events.description: On View: July 11 – September 23 Opening Reception: July 11th, 5pm to 8pm Continuing a tradition at the Morean, we are proud to present last year’s Margaret Murphy Steward Best of Show winner, Neverne Covington. In addition to a cash prize, the winning artist receives a solo show in our gallery to coincide with the following year’s members show. Neverne is the 14th artist to receive this opportunity. About Neverne: Neverne Covington’s drawings, prints, paintings and sculpture explore childhood, language, landscape, memory, and the gothic south. Her work has been exhibited at the Tampa Museum of Art, the Brevard 7278227872

events.title: Tarpon Springs Sponge Docks Craft Festival
events.description: The historic Sponge Docks of Tarpon Springs will once again come alive with creativity and community spirit as the Tarpon Springs Sponge Docks Craft Festival returns Saturday and Sunday, July 11 & 12, Presented by The Tarpon Springs Merchant's Association, produced by SIK Promotions, Inc., this event features a curated showcase of handmade arts and crafts, unique gifts, and artisan goods from regional and national makers. The Tarpon Springs Sponge Docks location provides a valuable platform for local and traveling artisans to display and sell their work while attracting thousands of visitors to the area. 727-322-5217

events.title: Business Banking 101: Business Funding Products
events.description: Join us for a free educational business banking workshop and networking event for local business owners, entrepreneurs, freelancers, consultants, real estate professionals, and community professionals. This session will help attendees better understand business banking products, funding options, and how to prepare before applying for capital. Topics include business credit and lending products, lines of credit, credit card funding options, equipment financing, revenue-based funding, alternative lending options, working capital solutions, and what banks and lenders typically look for. Attend in person at Riverview Library or online via Microsoft Teams. In-person seating is limited to 20 attendees. 813-563-5668

events.title: Workshop: Creative Cyanotype Printing with Lance Rothstein
events.description: Learn to make beautiful cyanotype prints at the Morean Darkroom. Film photography guru Lance Rothstein will walk you through the steps of coating the special emulsion and making breathtaking cyanotype photographs and botanical prints during this 3.5 hour workshop. Each participant can make at least two botanical prints and two photographs. All materials provided, but feel free to bring some interesting flowers, plant stems or leaves if you have them. A 7278227872

events.title: The High & Dry Festival 2026 Summer Edition
events.description: The High & Dry Festival is a one-of-a-kind celebration of functional beverages — from cannabis infused elixirs to adaptogenic blends and beyond. Enjoy unlimited tastings, hands-on workstations, and conversations with the brands and thought leaders shaping the future of mindful drinking. Whether you're sober-curious, wellness-focused, or just here to sip and discover, this weekend is your invitation to explore the intersection of flavor, function, and good vibes.

events.title: MEMORY LANE -- a SAGES Play with Purpose about Caregiving
events.description: Memory Lane is a heartfelt “Play with Purpose” by SAGES Theater exploring the emotional journey of caregiving for a loved one with memory loss and dementia. Through humor, tenderness, and true-to-life stories performed by senior actors, audiences experience the challenges, resilience, love, and hope woven throughout caregiving. Blending laughter and tears, this FREE family-friendly theatrical event sparks meaningful conversations about aging with grace while reminding caregivers they are not alone. Caregiver resources, goody bags, prize drawings, and local organizations are part of the experience. General admission seating. Reservations recommended. 727-202-2302
